title: trichilia sinensis
intro: Trichilia sinensis, also known as the Chinese Bitter Orange or Huang Pi, is a plant species native to China and Southeast Asia. It is a deciduous tree with a rich history of use in traditional medicine, valued for its bitter and astringent properties. Trichilia sinensis has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the plant are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to support digestive health and boost the immune system.
